sentences.text: Hey there, Kayla.
Can you hear me?
Ok.
Hello?
Yes, I can.
How are you this afternoon?
I'm doing good.
How are you?
I'm all right.
I'm all right for a Tuesday.
Um, looking forward to the holiday break coming up, I guess in a week and a half or so.
But, uh, coming up quick.
Yeah.
Yeah, and looks like Aaron's joined the meeting as well.
I think we're just waiting on audio from him.
Um, but thanks so much for, for scheduling a time for us to chat.

Um, as far as agenda for today, I wanted to learn a little bit about your role at plenty of places and kind of where, uh, apartment IQ was being used, kind of where it was falling short and then see if I can show you guys and enable a couple of features that might be more helpful over the next couple of months while we're under contract.
Does that sound good?
Yeah, definitely.
Um, so with plenty of places, I am the marketing director, um, I've been with plenty of places for almost nine years now.
Um, I started as a leasing agent and then I managed a couple of properties and then I got into the marketing role.
Um, so I went on maternity leave earlier this year and then they hired, uh, Erin and Drake who does our social media, um, while I was on maternity leave came back and, and got the ball rolling.

So, um, as far as apartment IQ goes, um, we were using it when we were pulling, uh, you know, market surveys and we really liked the, uh rent recommendations tab just to kind of see where we were in line with the other um with our comps.
But I don't know that we were all um you know, the managers at the properties that we had it for and, and us, I don't know that we were fully utilizing it to um its full capacity, I think.
Um I, I mean, I know that Aaron was doing a lot of the market surveys, so I think he was just more so going to apartments.com.
But um I could be wrong.
Hey, I'm sorry, I just figured out my um audio, I'm here now.
Nice to hear from you, Erin.
How are you doing?
I'm doing well.
How about you, Phil?
Not bad.
Not bad.
I know it's been a couple of months since we last connected but um thanks for taking the time to jump on.
Of course, of course.
Kay, I appreciate that background.
Uh So I guess the, you know, it's, it sounds like we were kind of using apartment IQ.
Um But usage was maybe the big issue.
Does, does that sound fair to say perhaps usage at the on site on the on site level?
Um And maybe a at, at your level as well?
Yeah.
Yeah, definitely got it.
And then Aaron, go ahead.
Yeah, go
ahead.
Aaron.
I was gonna say um yeah, I was pretty big on apartment IQ to start, but as I was using it, um honestly didn't find, I didn't find that it really gave any like good insights.
You know, most of the time I was like, oh, I kind of already know this, you know, I just glance at it and then do my more uh in depth search on apartments dot
com.
Got it.
And what are you, what were you finding in apartments.com?
What were you looking for?
Apartments.com that you felt?
You didn't get an apartment IQ
um like specials, how they presented their units, um
how they describe their units, stuff like that.
Just overall presentation.
What level of apartments.com um tier they're on though?
Of course, if you're on a gold tier and most of us are silver tier, we're not gonna be as high on the search results and we have that account, stuff like that.
Got it.
Ok.
That makes sense and specials like concessions or whatever kind of um perhaps fee give backs they're doing.
Yes, fair enough, fair enough.
And Katie, can you hear us?
All right, I I saw you just entered the room?
Yeah, hi.
Sorry for the
delay.
No, no problem.
Thanks for joining us.
Uh I was just kind of getting a little bit of a rundown from Kayla and Erin uh about apartment IQ and how it was fitting in at plenty of places or, or perhaps better phrased how it was falling short.
Um Would love to hear any color you could provide.
I know we spoke this past summer, so I'd love to hear kind of how things have changed since we last spoke.
Yeah.
Um I'm not as, you know, obviously looking at it as, as often as Aaron and Kayla are.
Um but I know that when I do pull it, I, you know, it's kind of the same thing, right?

I never really, I always kind of question because it's pulled off, you know, kind of the average what, you know, what, what the rents really, really are for our comps because, you know, going up permanent Q versus apartment, you know, apartments.com, I know that you guys are pulling that information, but sometimes when we call our comps, we're getting different information.
Um And also I think, you know, our, our managers unfortunately are under utilizing it.
Um You know, because they do have such great resources like Erin, like Kayla, um you know, like just pulling apartments.com building that report and calling those, you know, those properties.
So I, I think it's a good product.

I think it's just maybe not something that we, uh you know, fully utilize to our, to our fullest stability, but also, you know, nothing that's potentially groundbreaking in order for us to, you know, review pricing and things like that.
Sure.
No, that makes a lot of sense.
Um You know, I, I think I'm getting a pretty decent kind of picture about where we're falling short.
Um, especially, I mean, if you're going back to calling your comp straight up, that's definitely not, uh, we're, we're not using IQ the way we want to be.
Right.
Um, and then, you know, when I hear that we're calling our comps again and that we're not taking the, the market surveys as, as helpful as they are.
Um, where are you thinking you're seeing most of the difference?
What are you getting when you call your comp versus when you see apartment IQ?
I'd just like to drill into that just a little bit.
So I could really understand where the delta is between what we surface and what you're expecting to see or what you'd like to hear.
Sure.

You know, again, I, I, this is just from my experience and I've just, you know, done it a few times, um, over the last couple months, but, you know, um, apartment IQ, let's say, you know, it'll say Arvada Village, you know, has a rent of 1515, let's say, and then when I go on apartments.com, it's, you know, 1505 to 1705.
And then when we call them, they say, oh no, you know, our, our one budget or, you know, our, our units renting for 1525.
So we're just getting so much different information that, you know, it's already hard to kind of weed through to figure out what's truly what our our comps are charging.
Um And then getting the average kind of thrown at us and just all this different stuff, it's just kind of making it hard to weed through that, answers
your question.
No, definitely, it definitely does.
Um I think it requires a little bit of, of kind of the idea behind department IQ we're looking to make things uniform and we're looking to make things consistent.
Um So let me do a quick screen share and highlight some of the changes that we've, that we've made.
Um And what to do that?
I'm first going to pull up your account,
open up Google Chrome here.
All right.
Can everybody see your account?
Yeah, that should be coming through.
Ok.
So in your account just to kind of refresh what we've got going on here, these standard tables.
I would like to make a couple of changes and to do that, I'm going to just enable some of the premium tier features that we've brought on.
Um including our concessions, our n er we are about to go live with a market survey and we're about to go live with new executive reports.
So these things should potentially make a difference here.
I'm gonna go ahead and refresh the screen
and we're gonna see this change a bit.
We still have a recommendations tab on the right.
You'll see a new tab in the middle called data reports.
This is where we'll find all the traditional property tabs, the unit type tabs and the units
changes that we made this.
At the end of the summer, we added this concessions field where we're pulling concessions off websites because we can pull the concession detail.
And if it applies to rent, we can develop an average concession value and then plug that right into an average N er
for all these properties
and you'll find that that continues all the way through down to the units tab where we've got the average N er listed and any concessions that are relevant.
See if we could find some.
Here we go.
Uh So hovering your mouse over that lets you know exactly what's going on here.
We've also made some changes to how we highlight for you, how we're kind of calculating some of these rents
whenever we pull a minimum maximum rent, we're always talking about the lowest rent offered.
We hear oftentimes from folks that, that is what they want to see.
They only care about the lowest rent offered whether that's a 12 month lease or 15 month lease, we can standardize it for 12 months if that's something you'd prefer.
But right now when I say this is the max rent for a studio at Warrant Club.
What I mean is that among all the studios they currently have available?
That is the most expensive of the cheapest rents offered.
Does that make sense?
Yes, sometimes I have a hard time explaining that myself.
Um So that is how we're always pulling the lowest value.
Now, the other big change that we're actually gonna start going live with this week and the following week is this market survey tab,
we're slowly releasing it in an open beta.
It should be live for customers at the end of December.
But what we've done here with our market survey tab is create a view that is a lot more familiar for on-site folks, especially those who've been in the industry for quite a while.
Uh because we're able to pull a very familiar layout here
in addition to our regular properties tab where we can kind of pull just one specific property or a group of properties that are most relevant for us, perhaps a core set versus the market at large.
We also have our customizing of the metrics here
and some metrics.
We're particularly excited to highlight here for you are leases in the last three days, leases in the last seven days and then leases per week.
Uh Sunday to Sunday.
We also have new available units last seven days, last 30 days and new available units per week
because a lot of times we're often hearing, hey, I need to be able to calculate kind of a leasing velocity.
I need to understand how quickly we're putting units back on.
We're hoping that these changes make it a lot more uh a lot easier to get the metrics that you need and that your teams might need to make decisions.
We'll also have any concessions listed here as well in this concessions tab.
So I know you mentioned that you're using apartment IQ to get some of this information.
I'm not sure how far this goes perhaps to closing the Delta there.
But I would love to maybe let you guys run with some of these features and just see how that changes.
Um We've also made some changes to our PDF report, not sure how relevant the PDF reports have been for your team.
Um But we are able to schedule these, right?
So we can schedule these out to hit our on site teams inboxes regularly.
Um Whether that's weekly, monthly or quarterly, depending on how often you guys are meeting for pricing calls or anything like that.
But the change here is going to allow us to deliver.
Where is that downloads file?
Let me download that again.
Sorry about that.
But it's gonna allow us to generate a report that has trend information that might make it more digestible.
A lot of this information, right?
So the typical, you've got your standard image here.
It's branded for plenty of places.
It has the date that the data was collected on.
Um Then we'll have this general context page which is pretty similar from before, but we've now built in more of the relevant uh metrics that we've just added.
So our average concessions, average n er n er per square foot average rent.
Um We're also highlighting every single floor plan available in your market rather than just the ones that are available at your property.

That was a positive change we made so that you guys can see the totality of the market and then on our final page um that's not loading here because this is still in beta right now will be one concessions page that in one quick sheet.
You can see all the concessions that are live in a in a given market survey.
How interesting does this sound so far?
Am I hitting any cords yet or are we kind of feeling like this is not really moving the needle for us?
I find it intriguing personally, but I don't know, I have a feeling even though I don't really dive into rent comp as much as I used to.
Um but and I do more on request.
I have a feeling I'll still want to go to the source, which is apartments.com where we do primarily all of our marketing versus here.
But I am curious to how you guys calculate.
Um exactly like how many leases are um getting done?
Is that just seeing what gets off the market and you're assuming it's a lease?
Yeah.
So when we find a unit come off the website, we are considering that a lease.
Yeah.
Ok.
And then we're just timing that over the last 30 last seven.
same thing with new availability as something comes on, we're considering it newly available.
Um and then some stuff that happened to the back end uh stuff that you don't see immediately here, we launch a daily unit sync.
Obviously, if we're not feeling that kind of uh confidence in the data itself, this may not be as relevant.
Um But if we feel comfortable with the data, we are pushing it now to a variety of data, warehousing tools and, and intelligence tools.
So if this is something your team is using, we can enable that as well.
Uh And then going forward, we'll be exploring the amenities and fees and deposits kind of reconfiguring how we calculate that, talking, things like pet fees, uh parking fees, things like that.
And then we're exploring leasing traffic as a, as a metric as well.
Um So that's kind of what's going on at apartment IQ in the long term.
Glad to have showed you some of the changes that we're making here.
Um If you do find that there are a lot of differences, we also launch to suggest an update feature here.
So you can go ahead and see, hey, I feel like at a Cedar Ridge, I saw a uh the rent being XYZ.
I actually think it's ABC I can go ahead and change that data point, provide the update and any context around that.

And then our teams are gonna go ahead and validate that for you and make sure that uh it's something we missed and that we can reconfigure our scrapers um or see if there's a reason why apartment IQ is calculating it the way we are.
Um,
and then I guess last thing, you know, before, now that I have you all here, um, we're calling around and, and we're kind of considering apartment I, uh, apartments.com.
The source is that fair to say that we really feel strongly that Apartment I apartments.com is a source of truth.
I would say so.
Yes, I think so.
Yeah.
Most of our marketing
dollars on.
Mhm.
And I ask, I ask about that because oftentimes we'll see maybe a difference between what's offered at apartments.com and what's on websites.
Are you seeing a difference between what's on apartments.com and what are on your comp websites?
Um, or is the difference more so in when you're calling them and hearing a different number?
Well, I guess Katie is the one that primarily calls around.
I usually just go straight to apartments.com.
So I don't think I can answer that question personally.
Mhm.
Well, I, I asked because, you know, when there's that human element.
There's, there's the,
a lot of the reason people come to apartment IQ.
Right.
It's to standardize everything.
It's because they've done the call arounds, they feel that it's accurate to an extent.
Um, but with apartment IQ, we're pulling everything the same way.
Uh, everybody's speaking the same language when you're talking about apartment IQ data.
Right?
We can't make anything up.
Uh We're pulling directly from what a landlord has posted on their website and thus what renters are actually act, acting on and making decisions on.
So that has been some of the key success about apartment IQ and the reason that a lot of our customers love us.
Um So I'd love to, you know, give you guys the keys to this, take it for a spin, maybe we touch base in another couple of weeks, see how it's been working for you.
Um If not, if it's not working, then, you know, I'm, I'm happy to collect that feedback and bring it to my product team.
But uh I'd love to maybe give it one more shot here and see if this will be, this will be helpful for you guys,
for sure.
Yeah.
Yeah, I think that's fair.
And I really like one of the biggest things to me was the concessions too.
So I really like that you guys have rolled that out and are including that now.
And this market survey, like you said is so much more familiar to me than, you know, looking through all the different tabs.
So, um, yeah, we'll definitely continue to, to look at it and, you know, see how it works for us and follow up in a couple of weeks and see what we can do.
That sounds great, Kayla.
Um I really appreciate the time guys.
Uh Thank you for letting me chat you ear off for 20 minutes here.
I'll let you get back to it and um, we'll talk soon.
All right, I'll shoot you guys an email shortly just to kind of see how things are going and then maybe we set a touch 0.4 weeks from now.
Ok.
Ok, sounds good.
Thank you so much.
Thank you so much guys.
Take care, have a great rest of the week.
All right.
Happy holidays to you.
Bye now.

conversation.title: Plenty of Places Management (HQ) - Apartment IQ Connect
